questions.q: The Sun Temple of Konark is located in which present-day Indian state?
questions.explanation: Konark lies on the coast of Odisha (formerly Orissa) in eastern India.

questions.q: The Konark temple is dedicated to which deity?
questions.explanation: The temple was built to honour Surya, the Hindu sun god.

questions.q: The Sun Temple is famously conceived in the form of what?
questions.explanation: It was designed as a gigantic stone chariot of the sun god Surya.

questions.q: In which century CE was the Konark Sun Temple built?
questions.explanation: The temple was constructed in the 13th century CE.

questions.q: Which ruler built the Konark Sun Temple?
questions.explanation: The Eastern Ganga king Narasimhadeva I commissioned the temple in the 13th century.

questions.q: The Konark Sun Temple is recognised by UNESCO as what?
options: A Wonder of the Modern World
questions.explanation: The Konark Sun Temple is a UNESCO World Heritage Site.

questions.q: The stone chariot of Konark is famously shown being drawn by carved figures of which animals?
questions.explanation: The chariot is depicted as being pulled by a team of richly carved horses.

questions.q: The chariot form of the temple is supported by how many great carved stone wheels (per common count)?
questions.explanation: The temple base bears twenty-four intricately carved wheels, twelve pairs flanking the structure.

questions.q: Because of its dark appearance, European sailors historically nicknamed Konark as what?
questions.explanation: Mariners called Konark the 'Black Pagoda', contrasting it with a 'White Pagoda' at Puri.

questions.q: Konark stands close to which other major Odishan pilgrimage town?
questions.explanation: Konark lies near Puri, home of the famous Jagannath temple, on the Odisha coast.

questions.q: Narasimhadeva I, who built Konark, belonged to which dynasty?
questions.explanation: Narasimhadeva I was a ruler of the Eastern Ganga dynasty of Odisha.

questions.q: The carved wheels of the Konark chariot are widely interpreted as functioning as what?
questions.explanation: The spoked wheels can be read as sundials, with the shadows indicating the time of day.

questions.q: The Konark temple is built in which regional style of Hindu temple architecture?
questions.explanation: Konark is a masterpiece of the Kalinga school of temple architecture native to Odisha.

questions.q: The towering main sanctuary (deul) of Konark is today largely in what condition?
questions.explanation: The great main tower has long since collapsed; mainly the hall (jagamohana) and base survive.

questions.q: The surviving large pillared hall in front of the sanctum at Konark is known as the?
questions.explanation: The jagamohana, the porch or assembly hall, is the most prominent surviving structure.

questions.q: The temple's design aligns so that the rising sun's rays would reach which part?
options: The crown of the highest spire
The image of Surya in the sanctum
questions.explanation: The temple was oriented so that the first sunrays would fall upon the sun god's image in the sanctum.

questions.q: The word 'Konark' is derived from terms meaning what?
questions.explanation: Konark combines 'kona' (corner/angle) and 'arka' (sun), fitting its solar dedication.

questions.q: Konark's sculptures are noted for richly depicting which range of subjects?
options: Daily life, dance, and erotic motifs
questions.explanation: Its carvings cover deities, musicians, dancers, animals, and sensuous figures typical of medieval Odishan art.

questions.q: Narasimhadeva I is traditionally said to have built Konark partly to commemorate what?
options: His marriage to a foreign princess
The founding of a new religion
questions.explanation: Tradition links the temple's construction to celebrating Eastern Ganga military victories, including over the Bengal Sultanate's forces.

questions.q: A famous annual cultural event held against the backdrop of the temple is the?
questions.explanation: The Konark Dance Festival celebrates classical Indian dance with the temple as its setting.

questions.q: Konark, Puri, and Bhubaneswar together are often grouped as Odisha's what?
questions.explanation: These three centres form Odisha's celebrated 'Golden Triangle' of temple architecture.

questions.q: Konark Sun Temple was inscribed as a UNESCO World Heritage Site in which year?
questions.explanation: UNESCO inscribed the Konark Sun Temple on the World Heritage List in 1984.

questions.q: Narasimhadeva I built Konark; his reign is dated to approximately which span?
questions.explanation: Narasimhadeva I of the Eastern Gangas reigned roughly from 1238 to 1264 CE, when Konark was built.

questions.q: The number of pairs of wheels (twelve) on the chariot is often interpreted as representing what?
options: The twelve months of the year
questions.explanation: The twelve pairs of wheels are commonly read as symbolising the twelve months, marking the passage of time.

questions.q: The carved horses drawing the Konark chariot are usually counted as numbering how many?
questions.explanation: Seven horses pull the chariot, often interpreted as the seven days of the week or seven colours/rays of the sun.

questions.q: The original presiding image of Surya at Konark is most often described as carved from what?
questions.explanation: Surviving Surya images from Konark are of finely polished dark chlorite (greenstone), not metal or marble.

questions.q: In the early 20th century, British administrators famously filled the surviving jagamohana hall with what to stabilise it?
questions.explanation: To prevent collapse, the hall's interior was filled with sand and sealed in 1903 under British conservation efforts.

questions.q: The architect traditionally associated in legend with completing Konark is named?
options: Bisu Maharana (and the boy Dharmapada legend)
questions.explanation: Odishan legend names master builder Bisu Maharana, with the story of his son Dharmapada solving the final problem.

questions.q: The collapse of Konark's main tower is variously attributed to which causes?
options: Structural and foundation weakness, possibly aided by later damage
questions.explanation: Scholars attribute the tower's fall to structural/foundation problems, with debate over additional natural or human factors.

questions.q: Konark's loss of the navigational 'Black Pagoda' landmark is noted because mariners had used it for what?
options: A lighthouse with a live flame
questions.explanation: European sailors used the dark temple silhouette as a landmark for navigating the Bay of Bengal coast.
